# Swarm Project: Kapow

Delegates to the global `/swarm` workflow with Kapow-specific configuration
injected. Run `/swarm-project` exactly as you would `/swarm`.

## Usage

- `/swarm-project` — work all `bd ready` issues
- `/swarm-project 3` — max 3 concurrent teammates
- `/swarm-project 3 id-abc id-def` — specific issues, max 3 concurrent

## Instructions

Run the global `/swarm` skill, but override Phase 0 discovery with the
project config below. Do NOT read `.claude/swarm-config.md` — everything
is already here.

---

## Project Config (replaces Phase 0)

### Quality Gates (per teammate)

Choose based on files changed:

| Changed | Command |
|---|---|
| Go only | `make api-test-unit && make api-lint` |
| Web only | `cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test` |
| Both | `make test-standard` |
| GraphQL schema | Run `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync` **before** other gates |

### Full Quality Gate (Phase 5, on main)

```bash
make test-standard
```

### Post-Merge Hooks

If the merged branch touched any `graph/schema/*.graphql` file:

```bash
make api-generate && make web-schema-sync
```

If the demo walkthrough (`demo/steps.ts`) references changed UI, update it.

### Branch Naming

`worktree/<agent-name>` — use the teammate's name, not the issue ID.

### Git Workflow

Merge directly to `main` from the command line. Do not create pull requests.

○ kapow-q4u · E2E: Playwright infrastructure & tagging convention   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-07 · Updated: 2026-03-07

DESCRIPTION
Set up the E2E test infrastructure that all feature-specific test issues depend on.

## Work items

- Add `webServer` block to `web/playwright.config.ts` (auto-start dev server, `reuseExistingServer: true`)
- Create `e2e/helpers.ts` with shared utilities:
  - `searchWith(page, params)` — navigate to `/search` with URL params
  - `waitForResults(page)` — wait for skeleton to clear, results visible
  - `getResultCount(page)` — parse displayed result count
- Document tagging convention: each test title includes `@tier @feature` tags
  - Tiers: `@critical`, `@standard`, `@comprehensive`
  - Features: `@search`, `@filters`, `@pagination`, `@sorting`, `@views`, `@detail`, `@columns`, `@geo`, `@blend`, `@export`, `@auth`, `@nav`, `@responsive`
- Add `web-test-e2e` and `web-test-e2e-critical` targets to root Makefile
- Wire `web-test-e2e` into `test-full` target
- Update existing smoke tests to use the new tagging convention

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm test:e2e --grep @critical` runs only critical-tier tests
- `pnpm test:e2e --grep @search` runs only search-feature tests
- `make web-test-e2e` works from repo root
- Helpers importable from `e2e/helpers.ts`

@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
+
+/**
+ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
+ *
+ * Tagging convention for test titles:
+ *
+ *   Tiers (pick one):
+ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
+ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
+ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
+ *
+ *   Features (pick one or more):
+ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
+ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
+ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
+ *     @responsive
+ *
+ *   Example test title:
+ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
+ *
+ * @example
+ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
+ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
+ */
+export async function searchWith(
+  page: Page,
+  params: Record<string, string>,
+): Promise<void> {
+  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
+  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
+  await page.goto(url)
+}
+
+/**
+ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
+ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
+ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
+ */
+export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
+  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
+  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
+    timeout: 30_000,
+  })
+
+  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
+  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
+  await expect(
+    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
+  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
+}
+
+/**
+ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
+ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
+ *
+ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
+ */
+export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
+  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
+  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
+
+  if (!text) {
+    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
+  }
+
+  // Match "100+ results" format
+  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
+  if (plusMatch) {
+    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
+  }
+
+  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
+  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
+  if (ofMatch) {
+    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
+  }
+
+  throw new Error(`Could not parse result count from text: "${text}"`)
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
+
+/**
+ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
+ *
+ * Tagging convention for test titles:
+ *
+ *   Tiers (pick one):
+ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
+ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
+ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
+ *
+ *   Features (pick one or more):
+ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
+ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
+ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
+ *     @responsive
+ *
+ *   Example test title:
+ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
+ *
+ * @example
+ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
+ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
+ */
+export async function searchWith(
+  page: Page,
+  params: Record<string, string>,
+): Promise<void> {
+  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
+  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
+  await page.goto(url)
+}
+
+/**
+ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
+ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
+ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
+ */
+export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
+  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
+  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
+    timeout: 30_000,
+  })
+
+  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
+  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
+  await expect(
+    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
+  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
+}
+
+/**
+ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
+ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
+ *
+ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
+ */
+export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
+  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
+  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
+
+  if (!text) {
+    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
+  }
+
+  // Match "100+ results" format
+  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
+  if (plusMatch) {
+    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
+  }
+
+  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
+  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
+  if (ofMatch) {
+    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
+  }
+
+  throw new Error(`Could not parse result count from text: "${text}"`)
+}

Now I have all the information I need to identify the issue. Let me verify my understanding:

## Findings

### Root Cause: Environment Variable Leakage from .env File

The test is failing because **Vite automatically loads environment variables from the `.env` file during test execution**, and those variables are already present in `import.meta.env` when the test runs.

**Evidence:**

1. **The .env file contains Supabase credentials** (lines 46-48 of `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.env`):
   ```
   VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://vnsieofmefhwfnebbzcs.supabase.co
   VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
   ```

2. **Vite loads .env files automatically** into `import.meta.env`, regardless of whether you're running tests, dev, or build. This is standard Vite behavior — all `.env*` files are loaded and interpolated into the build process.

3. **The supabase.ts module reads at module load time**:
   ```typescript
   const supabaseUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL as string | undefined
   const supabaseAnonKey = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as string | undefined
   export const supabaseEnabled = Boolean(supabaseUrl && supabaseAnonKey)
   ```
   These variables are evaluated **at import time**, not at test time.

4. **The test tries to work around this**:
   - Line 11: `vi.resetModules()` — clears the module cache
   - Line 16: `await import('./supabase')` — re-imports the module
   
   However, **the re-import still gets the env vars from Vite's preloaded .env file**, so `supabaseEnabled` evaluates to `true` instead of `false`.

### Why the Current Fix Doesn't Work

The test expects to import a clean module with no env vars set, but:
- `vi.resetModules()` clears JavaScript's module cache
- It does NOT reset Vite's `import.meta.env` object, which is a statically transformed property at build time
- The `.env` file is loaded by Vite's config at startup and injected into the build context
- When the module re-imports, it reads the same pre-loaded env vars again

### The Correct Fix

There are two viable approaches:

**Option 1: Mock import.meta.env before re-importing** (Recommended)
```typescript
it('provides a no-op stub when env vars are missing', async () => {
  vi.stubGlobal('import.meta', {
    env: {
      VITE_SUPABASE_URL: undefined,
      VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: undefined,
    }
  })
  
  const { supabase, supabaseEnabled } = await import('./supabase')
  expect(supabaseEnabled).toBe(false)
  // ... rest of test
})
```

**Option 2: Configure Vitest to exclude .env loading for tests** (Alternative)
Add to `vitest.config.ts`:
```typescript
test: {
  environment: 'happy-dom',
  env: {
    VITE_SUPABASE_URL: '',
    VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: '',
  },
}
```
This explicitly overrides env vars for test execution only.

The root issue is that **`import.meta.env` is Vite's compile-time environment variable injection mechanism** — it's not a standard Node.js `process.env` that can be easily modified at runtime. The variables are baked in during the build/transform phase.

   277→	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \

   279→	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \

   284→		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
   285→			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
   286→				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
   287→					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
   288→					break; \
   289→				fi; \
   290→			done; \
   291→		done; \
   292→	echo ""; \
   293→	echo "All targets:"; \
   294→	echo ""; \
   295→	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
   296→		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
   297→			SKIP=false; \
   298→			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
   299→				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
   300→			done; \
   301→			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
   302→				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
   303→			fi; \
   304→		done

1→## Kapow API Makefile
     2→## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     3→## Usage: make <target>
     4→## Targets requiring DATABASE_URL: migrate-up, migrate-down, migrate-status, migrate-create
     5→
     6→BINARY      := bin/kapow-api
     7→CMD         := ./cmd/server
     8→MODULE      := github.com/ketang/kapow/api
     9→VERSION     := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)
    10→BUILD_TIME  := $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    11→LDFLAGS     := -ldflags "-X $(MODULE)/internal/version.Version=$(VERSION) \
    12→                          -X $(MODULE)/internal/version.BuildTime=$(BUILD_TIME)"
    13→
    14→GOLANGCI_LINT := $(shell which golangci-lint 2>/dev/null || echo $(shell go env GOPATH)/bin/golangci-lint)
    15→
    16→GOOSE_DRIVER  := postgres
    17→GOOSE_DBSTRING ?= $(DATABASE_URL)
    18→GOOSE_DIR     := migrations
    19→
    20→.PHONY: build build-full run genenum generate lint vet fmt tidy clean \
    21→        migrate-up migrate-down migrate-status migrate-create \
    22→        test test-unit test-integration dev schema-gen help
    23→
    24→## build: Compile the server binary into bin/kapow-api
    25→build:
    26→	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BINARY) $(CMD)
    27→
    28→## build-full: Build with embedded web frontend (requires web/dist/ to exist)
    29→build-full:
    30→	rm -rf ui/dist
    31→	cp -r ../web/dist ui/dist
    32→	go build $(LDFLAGS) -tags ui -o $(BINARY) $(CMD)
    33→	rm -rf ui/dist
    34→
    35→## run: Run the server directly (no build step)
    36→run:
    37→	go run $(LDFLAGS) $(CMD)
    38→
    39→## genenum: Generate SearchFieldName enum from registry
    40→genenum:
    41→	go run ./cmd/genenum
    42→
    43→## generate: Re-run gqlgen code generation (runs genenum first)
    44→generate: genenum
    45→	go generate ./...
    46→
    47→## lint: Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec security checks)
    48→lint:
    49→	go vet ./...
    50→	$(GOLANGCI_LINT) run ./...
    51→
    52→## vet: Run go vet
    53→vet:
    54→	go vet ./...
    55→
    56→## fmt: Format all Go source files
    57→fmt:
    58→	gofmt -w .
    59→
    60→## tidy: Tidy go.mod and go.sum
    61→tidy:
    62→	go mod tidy
    63→
    64→## clean: Remove build artifacts
    65→clean:
    66→	rm -rf bin/
    67→
    68→## migrate-up: Apply all pending database migrations
    69→migrate-up:
    70→	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    71→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" up
    72→
    73→## migrate-down: Roll back the most recent migration
    74→migrate-down:
    75→	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    76→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" down
    77→
    78→## migrate-status: Show migration status
    79→migrate-status:
    80→	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    81→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" status
    82→
    83→## migrate-create NAME=<name>: Create a new migration file
    84→migrate-create:
    85→	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    86→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" create $(NAME) sql
    87→
    88→## test: Run all tests with race detector
    89→test:
    90→	go test -race ./...
    91→
    92→## test-unit: Run unit tests only (short mode)
    93→test-unit:
    94→	go test -race -short ./...
    95→
    96→## test-integration: Run integration tests (starts a container automatically; set TEST_DATABASE_URL to use an existing DB instead)
    97→test-integration:
    98→	go test -race -run Integration ./...
    99→
   100→## dev: Run with air hot-reload (requires: go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest)
   101→dev:
   102→	air
   103→
   104→## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
   105→schema-gen:
   106→	bash ../scripts/gen-schema.sh
   107→
   108→## help: List all available targets
   109→help:
   110→	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'
   111→

     4→FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder
     5→
     6→ARG VERSION=docker
     7→ARG BUILD_TIME
     8→
     9→WORKDIR /build
    10→
    11→# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
    12→COPY go.mod go.sum ./
    13→RUN go mod download
    14→
    15→COPY . .
    16→
    17→RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
    18→        -ldflags "-s -w \
    19→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
    20→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
    21→        -o /bin/kapow-api \
    22→        ./cmd/server
    23→
    24→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→# Stage 2: Minimal runtime
    26→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→FROM alpine:3.21
    28→
    29→RUN addgroup -S kapow && adduser -S kapow -G kapow
    30→
    31→COPY --from=builder /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api
    32→
    33→USER kapow
    34→
    35→EXPOSE 8081
    36→
    37→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    38→    CMD ["wget", "--quiet", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck"]
    39→
    40→ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/kapow-api"]
    41→

1→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Stage 1: Build the Go API binary
     3→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4→FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder-api
     5→
     6→ARG VERSION=docker
     7→ARG BUILD_TIME
     8→
     9→WORKDIR /build
    10→
    11→# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
    12→COPY api/go.mod api/go.sum ./
    13→RUN go mod download
    14→
    15→COPY api/ ./
    16→
    17→RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
    18→        -ldflags "-s -w \
    19→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
    20→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
    21→        -o /bin/kapow-api \
    22→        ./cmd/server
    23→
    24→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→# Stage 2: Build the web frontend
    26→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→FROM node:22-alpine AS builder-web
    28→
    29→WORKDIR /build
    30→
    31→# Install pnpm then fetch dependencies (layer caching)
    32→RUN npm install -g pnpm@latest
    33→
    34→COPY web/package.json web/pnpm-lock.yaml ./
    35→RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
    36→
    37→COPY web/ ./
    38→
    39→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_URL
    40→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
    41→RUN pnpm build
    42→
    43→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→# Stage 3: Runtime image — postgres+postgis, Go API, nginx, supervisord
    45→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→FROM postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine
    47→
    48→# Install runtime deps: nginx (web server + proxy), supervisor (process manager), xz (fixture decompression)
    49→# hadolint ignore=DL3018
    50→RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor xz
    51→
    52→# API binary
    53→COPY --from=builder-api /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api
    54→
    55→# Web assets served by nginx
    56→COPY --from=builder-web /build/dist /var/www/kapow
    57→
    58→# Config files
    59→COPY docker/nginx.conf      /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    60→COPY docker/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisord.conf
    61→COPY docker/entrypoint.sh   /entrypoint.sh
    62→
    63→# Fixture data — loaded on first boot
    64→COPY common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz /data/complete.sql.xz
    65→
    66→RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh \
    67→    && mkdir -p /run/nginx
    68→

## Summary of Findings

### 1. **Version Mismatch**
- **Installed version**: golangci-lint **v1.64.8** (built with Go 1.24.4)
- **Config format expectation**: **v2** (as defined by `version: "2"` in `api/.golangci.yml`)
- **Error**: When running `golangci-lint run`, the error is triggered: `"you are using a configuration file for golangci-lint v2 with golangci-lint v1: please use golangci-lint v2"`

### 2. **Configuration File Details**
- **Location**: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/.golangci.yml`
- **Content**: Line 1 declares `version: "2"` — this is a v2-only feature
- **v1 validation result**: The `golangci-lint config verify` command fails with: `jsonschema: "" does not validate with "/additionalProperties": additional properties 'version' not allowed`
- **What this means**: The `version` field itself is not valid in v1 configs; it's a v2 feature

### 3. **When the Config Was Created**
- **Commit**: `49a472d` ("Add static analysis to default build pipeline")
- **Date**: Feb 24, 2026
- **What changed**: The `.golangci.yml` was created with v2 format, enabling linters like gosec, bodyclose, errorlint, gocritic, and unconvert
- **Intent**: This was part of adding static analysis to the build pipeline, but the installed binary (v1) cannot read the v2 config format

### 4. **Root Cause**
The config file was created in v2 format, but:
- **No version pin exists in the codebase** (go.mod doesn't pin golangci-lint)
- **No .tool-versions or similar** exists to mandate v2
- **The installed system binary is v1**, which was likely installed before the config was upgraded

### 5. **Correct Fix**
There are two valid approaches (choose one):

**Option A (Recommended): Upgrade the installed binary to golangci-lint v2**
- Run: `go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest` (installs v2)
- This aligns with the v2 config already in the repo
- Future-proofs the project with modern linter capabilities

**Option B: Downgrade the config to v1 format**
- Remove the `version: "2"` line from `api/.golangci.yml`
- Keep the rest of the linter configuration as-is (v1 supports the same linters, just without the version field)
- Less ideal since it leaves the system on an older version long-term

### 6. **Why It Matters**
- The current state breaks `make lint` and `make test-standard` (which includes linting)
- golangci-lint v2 has better performance, more linters, and improved configuration options
- The config was intentionally written for v2, so upgrading the binary is the intended fix

### Files Involved
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/.golangci.yml` — v2 config format
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/Makefile` (line 50) — runs `golangci-lint run ./...`
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/Makefile` (line 51) — delegates to api lint target

    15→

    18→JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    19→
    20→# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
    21→# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
    22→
    23→# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)

Based on the search results, here are the key configuration format differences between golangci-lint v1 and v2:

## Major Structural Changes

**Version Declaration:** v2 configurations require a version field with the value "2".

**Linter Enable/Disable Options:** The enable-all and disable-all options have been replaced with a single, more intuitive option: linters.default. You can now set fast linters as the default set of linters using the linters.default: fast option to ensure that only the fastest linters are used by default.

**Settings Organization:** The linter settings have moved to the linters section. Additionally, settings for gci, gofmt, gofumpt, and goimports are moved to the formatters.settings section.

**Exclusions:** In v2, there are no exclusions by default, however, you now have the possibility to enable human-readable exclusion presets.

**Path References:** Inside some linters configuration that use paths, you can now use the placeholder ${base-path} to refer to the same default location as the golangci-lint options.

## Migration Tool

To ease the transition to v2, golangci-lint includes a migration command: golangci-lint migrate, which automatically converts your v1 configuration to the new v2 format. However, comments inside a configuration file are not migrated and need to be added manually after the migration.

Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Complete Analysis of the Supabase Test Failure

Based on my thorough investigation, here's the full picture of the issue:

### Current State

**Test File**: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/lib/supabase.test.ts`

**Test Status**: Currently PASSING (but fragile)

**Module Under Test**: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/lib/supabase.ts`

### The Problem Explained

The test is **passing now but is architecturally flawed**. Here's why:

#### 1. **Root Cause: Vite's Default Environment Variable Handling**

- **Vite automatically loads `.env` files** from the project root and makes them available via `import.meta.env`
- **Supabase vars are defined** in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.env`:
  ```
  VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://vnsieofmefhwfnebbzcs.supabase.co
  VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
  ```
- **`import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL` is injected at compile time**, not runtime
- This means the actual environment variables are **baked into the module when it's first imported**

#### 2. **Why the Test Currently Passes**

The first test (`provides a no-op stub when env vars are missing`) passes because:

1. `vi.resetModules()` in `beforeEach()` clears the module cache
2. When the module is re-imported with `await import('./supabase')`, Vitest re-evaluates it
3. **However**, Vite's environment variable resolution during module transformation happens at parse time, not import time
4. The test manually deletes properties from `import.meta.env` in the second test, and the resets work because `vi.resetModules()` clears all cached modules

**BUT this is fragile because**:
- It relies on the order of test execution
- `vi.resetModules()` + `await import()` pattern is brittle
- The real issue is that `import.meta.env` is meant to be replaced at build time, not at test time
- Different Vitest/Vite versions might handle this differently

#### 3. **Why This Is a Real Problem**

The fundamental issue:
- **`import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL` is NOT a real environment variable at runtime**
- It's a **compile-time constant that gets replaced during Vite's build/transform step**
- In `supabase.ts` (line 3-4):
  ```ts
  const supabaseUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL as string | undefined
  const supabaseAnonKey = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as string | undefined
  ```
  These get replaced with literal values or `undefined` during the Vite transformation

- In tests, when you try to manually set `import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL = 'value'` (line 26), you're setting it **after** the module code has already been transformed, which doesn't actually affect the already-transformed values

### Test Configuration Files

#### `web/vitest.config.ts`
- Uses `happy-dom` environment
- Has `setupFiles: ['./src/test/setup.ts']` for test utilities
- **No `envPrefix` or `env` configuration** (this is the key missing piece)
- No special handling for environment variable isolation per test

#### `web/vite.config.ts`
- Standard Vite config with React plugin and path alias
- **No special `envPrefix` configuration**
- Vite's default behavior: loads all `.env*` files and exposes ones with `VITE_` prefix

### How Other Tests Handle This

**Searched across all test files** (`web/src/**/*.test.ts`):
- `useWebMCP.test.ts` — mocks window global, no env handling
- `authStore.test.ts` — mocks Supabase module, no env manipulation
- No other tests in the codebase manipulate `import.meta.env`
- **`vi.stubEnv()` is NOT used anywhere** in the project

### The No-Op Test Pattern (lines 15-23)

The test expects:
1. `supabaseEnabled` to be `false` when env vars are "missing"
2. But it manually tries to delete them from `import.meta.env`
3. Since `vi.resetModules()` is called first, it clears the module cache
4. The re-import then happens fresh

**The issue**: This pattern works **by accident**, not by design:
- Vite transforms `import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL` into actual values
- Deleting properties from `import.meta.env` doesn't affect already-transformed code
- The reset-and-reimport pattern works because it forces re-transformation

### The Manual Env Set Pattern (lines 25-41)

Lines 26-27:
```ts
import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL = 'https://test.supabase.co'
import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY = 'test-anon-key'
```

This also **doesn't work as intended**:
- These assignments happen after module transformation
- They won't affect the module-level constants already initialized
- It works in practice because of the `vi.resetModules()` + `await import()` combo

# Golangci-lint Configuration: V1 vs V2

Based on the documentation provided, the page focuses on **v2 configuration only** and does not explicitly detail structural differences between v1 and v2 formats.

## What the Documentation Shows

The current configuration structure (v2) uses a hierarchical YAML/TOML/JSON format with these main sections:

```yaml
version: "2"
linters:
  default: all
  enable: [list of linters]
  disable: [list of linters]
  settings: {}
  exclusions: {}
formatters:
  enable: [list of formatters]
  settings: {}
  exclusions: {}
issues: {}
output: {}
run: {}
severity: {}
```

## Linters Configuration Example (V2)

The linters section supports:
- **Default sets**: `"standard"`, `"all"`, `"none"`, or `"fast"`
- **Selective enabling/disabling**: Individual linter control via `enable` and `disable` lists
- **Granular rules**: Path-based and text-based exclusion patterns with per-linter configuration

For historical v1 comparisons, the documentation references a **"[Documentation v1 ↗](https://golangci.github.io/legacy-v1-doc/)"** link, suggesting structural differences exist but aren't documented on this page.

# Migrating from golangci-lint v1 to v2

## Migration Steps

The easiest approach is using the built-in migration command:

```bash
golangci-lint migrate
```

This tool automatically converts your v1 configuration to v2 format. Note that "comments inside a configuration file are not migrated" and must be added manually afterward. You can also specify output format with `--format json` or `--format yaml`.

The command creates a backup file (`.bck` extension) before conversion and validates against the v1 JSON Schema unless you use `--skip-validation`.

## Key v2-Specific Changes

**Configuration Structure Reorganization:**
The monolithic `linters-settings` section splits into `linters.settings` and `formatters.settings`, with formatting tools like gci and gofmt moving to the formatters section.

**Linter Management:**
Replace `disable-all: true` with `default: none` and `enable-all: true` with `default: all`. The `fast` option becomes `default: fast` or the `--fast-only` flag.

**Consolidated Linters:**
Several linters merged—stylecheck, gosimple, and staticcheck are now under staticcheck only.

**Exclusion System:**
v2 introduces dedicated `linters.exclusions` and `formatters.exclusions` sections replacing scattered exclude options. These support `paths`, `rules`, `generated`, and `presets`.

**Output Formatting:**
The `--out-format` flag is replaced with granular `--output.<format>.<option>` flags (e.g., `--output.json.path=stdout`).

**Removed Features:**
Many deprecated v1 options vanish entirely—`typecheck` is not configurable, alternative linter names (gas→gosec) must update, and several specialized ignore options convert to exclusion rules.

# Fix Pre-existing Test Failures

## Context

`make test-standard` has two pre-existing failures unrelated to recent work:
1. **Supabase test** — env var leakage from `.env` into Vitest
2. **golangci-lint** — v1 binary installed but config is v2 format

Both break the CI quality gate and must be fixed for `make test-standard` to pass.

---

## Fix 1: Supabase test env leakage

**Root cause**: Vite auto-loads `.env` (which has real `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and
`VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`) into `import.meta.env`. The test at
`web/src/lib/supabase.test.ts` tries to test the "no env vars" path but the
vars are already injected.

**Fix**: Use `vi.stubEnv()` (Vitest's built-in API) to control env vars per test,
instead of manually deleting/setting properties on `import.meta.env`.

### Changes

**`web/src/lib/supabase.test.ts`**:
- In the "no-op stub" test: call `vi.stubEnv('VITE_SUPABASE_URL', '')` and
  `vi.stubEnv('VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY', '')` before the dynamic `import('./supabase')`
- In the "creates real client" test: call `vi.stubEnv('VITE_SUPABASE_URL', 'https://test.supabase.co')`
  and `vi.stubEnv('VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY', 'test-anon-key')` before the dynamic import
- Add `vi.unstubAllEnvs()` in `afterEach` for cleanup
- Remove manual `import.meta.env` property manipulation

---

## Fix 2: golangci-lint version mismatch

**Root cause**: `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` installs golangci-lint v1.64.8, but
`api/.golangci.yml` declares `version: "2"`. The config uses zero v2-specific
features — just 5 enabled linters and directory exclusions.

**Fix**: Upgrade golangci-lint to v2 in the Dockerfile. The config already works
with v2; no config changes needed. v1 is legacy.

### Changes

**`.devcontainer/Dockerfile`** (line 16):
- Change version from `v1.64.8` to latest stable v2 (e.g. `v2.1.6` or whatever
  `curl -sSfL ... | sh -s -- -b ... latest` resolves to)

---

## Verification

```bash
# After both fixes:
cd web && pnpm test -- src/lib/supabase.test.ts   # supabase tests pass
make api-lint                                       # golangci-lint runs clean
make test-standard                                  # full gate passes
```

`api/.golangci.yml` declares `version: "2"`. The config uses zero v2-specific
features — just 5 enabled linters and directory exclusions.

**Fix**: Upgrade golangci-lint to v2 in the Dockerfile. The config already works
with v2; no config changes needed. v1 is legacy.
